align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|42|18.38|N|135|13|35.41|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nishimotomezuka Kofun'''|西求女塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Miyakodori, neighborhood of [[Nada-ku, Kobe]], {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2005.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/218329|title=西求女塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nishimotomezuka Kofun is a {{ill|''{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}{{ill|zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because of the mass replacement error that gets rid of the links like this otherwise|lt=zenpō-kōhō-fun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, formed by two conjoined rectangles, when viewed from above. It is located on an {{ill|Alluvial fan|en|Alluvial fan|WD=Q465088|lt=Alluvial fan|1=Alluvial fan}} at an elevation of six to eight meters between the southern foot of {{ill|Mount Rokkō|en|Mount Rokkō|WD=Q462120|lt=Mount Rokkō|1=Mount Rokkō}} and {{ill|Osaka Bay|en|Osaka Bay|WD=Q999162|lt=Osaka Bay|1=Osaka Bay}} in the southeastern part of the prefecture. The existence of ancient Kofuns has been known since ancient times and is one of the several early Kofun period Kofuns distributed in the area. Together with the {{nihongo|[[Otomezuka Kofun]]|処女塚古墳||}} and the {{nihongo|Higashimotomezuka Kofun|東求女塚古墳}} it has been connected with the {{nihongo|Unai-otomo Legend|菟原処女の伝説||}}, which appears in the ''{{ill|Man'yōshū|en|Man'yōshū|WD=Q834321|lt=Man'yōshū|1=Man'yōshū}}'' and the ''{{ill|Yamato Monogatari|en|Yamato Monogatari|WD=Q1354541|lt=Yamato Monogatari|1=Yamato Monogatari}}'', a tragic love story in which a beautiful young maiden is distrait that two equally worthy suitors are fighting for her hand in marriage. Unable to choose between the two, she commits suicide and the two suitors both follow her into death.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Due to landslides and other natural disasters, notably the {{ill|1596 Keichō–Fushimi earthquake|en|1596 Keichō–Fushimi earthquake|WD=Q11495186|lt=1596 Keichō–Fushimi earthquake|1=1596 Keichō–Fushimi earthquake}}, the [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] of the tumulus collapsed, and the form of the tumulus became unclear. Traditionally, it was thought to be a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}} keyhole-shaped tumulus, but as a result of [[archaeological excavation]]s that have been continuously conducted since 1985, it was found that it was the largest two conjoined rectangle style tumulus in the early Kofun period. The tumulus was found to measure 98 meters in length, with a 52-meter rear portion, 46 meter anterior, 50 meters in width at the rear, 25 meters in width at the neck, and up to 9 meters in height. The main axis is orientated in the east–west direction, and there are two or more steps in the front part, and ''[[fukiishi]]'' were slightly detected near the base. The burial chamber was a pit-type stone chamber with an auxiliary chamber, containing a split bamboo-shaped coffin. Stone materials for the burial chamber were not only local but were also transported from {{ill|Awa Province (Tokushima)|en|Awa Province (Tokushima)|WD=Q907457|lt=Awa Province|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}} ({{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}) and {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province|1=Kii Province}} ({{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture|1=Wakayama Prefecture}}).<ref name="Shiseki"/>
{{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} included a total of 11 ''[[Shinju-kyo]]'' {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, including seven triangular-rimmed divine beast mirrors, of the type that the Chinese chronicle {{transliteration|zh|Wei zhi}} ({{lang|zh|魏志}} "Records of Wei"), which is part of the ''{{ill|Records of the Three Kingdoms|en|Records of the Three Kingdoms|WD=Q247318|lt=Records of the Three Kingdoms|1=Records of the Three Kingdoms}}'' ({{lang|zh|三國志}}), states that Emperor {{ill|Cao Rui|en|Cao Rui|WD=Q378470|lt=Cao Rui|1=Cao Rui}} sent to Queen [[Himiko]] of [[:en:Wa (Japan)|Wa]] in the year 239 {{ill|Common Era|en|Common Era|WD=Q208141|lt=CE|1=Common Era}}.{{sfnp|Goodrich|1951|p=15}} Two of the triangular-rimmed mirrors were found to be wrapped in cloth. The main room contained these bronze mirrors and jasper stone spindle-shaped artifacts, while the auxiliary room contained iron products such as 56 iron arrowheads, and 26 iron swords, iron daggers, axes, and fishing gear. From these grave goods, it is estimated that the tumulus was constructed before 250 CE.<ref name="Shiseki"/> In 2005, these artifacts collectively designated as a National {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/160121|title=兵庫県西求女塚古墳出土品|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref>
Currently, the tumulus is maintained as a park. It is located a two-minute walk from {{ill|Nishinada Station|en|Nishinada Station|WD=Q7040635|lt=Nishinada Station|1=Nishinada Station}} on the {{ill|Hanshin Electric Railway|en|Hanshin Electric Railway|WD=Q1140781|lt=Hanshin Electric Railway|1=Hanshin Electric Railway}} {{ill|Hanshin Main Line|en|Hanshin Main Line|WD=Q1053647|lt=Hanshin Main Line|1=Hanshin Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
